name: The Golden Rule
description: The principle of treating others as one wishes to be treated, found across all major religious traditions.

passages.text: Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the LORD.
But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you.
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate, who is free from egoism and the sense of 'mine,' who is equal in pain and pleasure, who is forgiving—such a devotee is dear to Me.
Youth is not lasting; it is but the time of a meeting of the senses. Seeing that this is so, a wise person should refrain from sense pleasures.

scienceNotes: Evolutionary biology and neuroscience support the Golden Rule's cross-cultural universality. Mirror neurons — discovered by Rizzolatti et al. (1996) — provide a neural basis for empathy, while game theory research (Nowak, 2006) shows that reciprocal altruism is an evolutionarily stable strategy. Developmental psychology (Tomasello, 2009) finds that prosocial behavior emerges in human infants before language, suggesting a biological foundation for the 'treat others as you wish to be treated' ethic.

name: Compassion & Empathy
description: The virtue of showing kindness, understanding, and mercy toward others, especially those who suffer.

passages.text: Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with an impure mind a person speaks or acts, suffering follows him like the wheel that follows the foot of the ox.
Or have they taken protectors [or allies] besides Him? For Allah — He is the Protector, and He gives life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate, who is free from egoism and the sense of 'mine,' who is equal in pain and pleasure, who is forgiving—such a devotee is dear to Me.
Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.
O mind, why do you wander lost, without your Beloved? Apply yourself to the Guru's Word, and the Lord shall come to your home.
passages.chapterTitle: Sukhmani Sahib — The Psalm of Peace (Guru Arjan)

scienceNotes: Controlled studies on Compassion-Focused Therapy (CFT) show significant reductions in depression, anxiety, and self-criticism (Leaviss & Uttley, 2015). fMRI research (Klimecki et al., 2013) reveals that compassion training activates the ventral striatum and orbitofrontal cortex — regions linked to reward and affiliation — distinct from empathy's pain-network activation. Loving-kindness meditation (LKM) interventions (Hofmann et al., 2011) demonstrate dose-dependent increases in positive affect and social connectedness, with effect sizes comparable to standard antidepressants.

name: Purpose of Life
description: Teachings about the meaning, goal, and purpose of human existence.

passages.text: Perform your prescribed duties, for action is superior to inaction. Even the maintenance of your body would not be possible through inaction.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
By time,
The best of paths is the Eightfold Path; the best of truths is the Four Noble Truths; the best of mental states is detachment; the best of men is the one with eyes to see.
Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

scienceNotes: Psychological research on meaning and purpose (Ryff & Singer, 1998; Steger, 2009) identifies eudaimonic well-being — living in accordance with one's deepest values — as a stronger predictor of health than hedonic happiness. Longitudinal studies (Hill & Turiano, 2014) show that purpose in life predicts reduced all-cause mortality across the lifespan, mediated by lower cortisol levels and improved cardiovascular regulation. Evolutionary accounts (Buss, 2008) frame purpose-seeking as an adaptive mechanism that organizes goal-directed behavior and fosters community cohesion.

name: Forgiveness
description: The act of pardoning others for wrongs committed and the divine forgiveness of sins.

passages.text: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you.
He who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate, who is free from egoism and the sense of 'mine,' who is equal in pain and pleasure, who is forgiving—such a devotee is dear to Me.
Or have they taken protectors [or allies] besides Him? For Allah — He is the Protector, and He gives life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.
Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with a pure mind a person speaks or acts, happiness follows him like his never-departing shadow.

scienceNotes: Forgiveness interventions (Worthington et al., 2015, meta-analysis of 54 studies) produce moderate-to-large reductions in depression (g = 0.58) and anxiety (g = 0.54). fMRI research (Ricciardi et al., 2013) shows that forgiving others activates the prefrontal cortex and dampens amygdala response, suggesting cognitive reappraisal as the underlying mechanism. The Enright Forgiveness Inventory (EFI) has been validated across 20+ cultures, supporting forgiveness as a cross-cultural construct with measurable health benefits — lower blood pressure, improved immune function (vanOyen Witvliet, 2001).

name: Afterlife & Salvation
description: Beliefs about what happens after death, including heaven, hell, rebirth, and liberation.

passages.text: For the soul there is neither birth nor death at any time. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.
For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
Does not man see that We created him from a sperm-drop? Yet he is a clear adversary.
Earnestness is the path of immortality, negligence the path of death. Those who are earnest do not die; those who are negligent are already dead.
I am Thine, O Lord, I am Thine; all mine are Thine, O Great King. My body, my breath, my wealth and my soul are Thine, O Lord; everything is Thine.
passages.chapterTitle: Anand Sahib — The Song of Bliss (Guru Amar Das)

passages.text: I call heaven and earth to record this day against you, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and cursing: therefore choose life, that both thou and thy seed may live.

scienceNotes: Near-Death Experience (NDE) research (van Lommel et al., 2001, Lancet; Greyson, 2021) documents consistent phenomenological reports across cultures — out-of-body perception, life review, encounter with light — occurring during cardiac arrest when clinical brain activity is absent. The AWARE studies (Parnia et al., 2014) found 2-12% of cardiac arrest survivors recall lucid experiences during EEG-silent periods. However, neurobiological explanations (oxidative stress, glutamate dysregulation, REM intrusion) remain viable. The question remains scientifically unresolved; the NDE evidence is suggestive but not conclusive, and the diversity of reported afterlifes (heaven, rebirth, annihilation) mirrors cultural expectations.

name: Charity & Generosity
description: The virtue of giving to those in need, including almsgiving, charity, and service to others.

passages.text: There is no compulsion in religion. The right course has become distinct from the wrong.
And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
Therefore, without attachment, perform always the work that has to be done; for by performing work without attachment, man verily reaches the Supreme.

And now, Israel, what doth the LORD thy God require of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all his ways, and to love him, and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul,
He is beyond time. He cannot be described. By hearing, seeing, and speaking, He is purified.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

scienceNotes: Behavioral economics experiments (Andreoni, 1990; Harbaugh, 1998) identify 'warm-glow giving' — the intrinsic reward of generosity — via fMRI activation in the ventral striatum and caudate nucleus. Charitable giving triggers stronger neural responses than receiving money (Moll et al., 2006). Longitudinal public health studies (Musick & Wilson, 2003) show that volunteers have 20-35% lower mortality risk over 5-8 year follow-ups. The 'helpers' high' — a physiological state of reduced cortisol and increased oxytocin — has been replicated in randomized controlled trials of prosocial behavior.

name: Peace & Non-Violence
description: Teachings about peace, non-violence, and resolving conflicts without harm.

passages.text: Youth is not lasting; it is but the time of a meeting of the senses. Seeing that this is so, a wise person should refrain from sense pleasures.
Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
He whose mind is untroubled in the midst of sorrows and is free from eager desire amid pleasures, he from whom passion, fear, and rage have passed away—he is called a sage of steady wisdom.

O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you.
Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

passages.text: The LORD bless thee, and keep thee:

scienceNotes: The science of peace spans neurobiology (peace meditation increases gamma coherence and telomerase activity — Davidson et al., 2003; Epel et al., 2009), conflict resolution psychology (contact hypothesis — Pettigrew & Tropp, 2006 meta-analysis of 515 studies — finds intergroup contact reduces prejudice with r = -0.21), and peace economics (Collier & Hoeffler, 2004 on the economic drivers of civil war). Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) protocols derived from contemplative peace traditions show effect sizes of d = 0.5-0.8 for anxiety reduction (Khoury et al., 2013).

name: Wisdom & Knowledge
description: The pursuit of wisdom, understanding, and spiritual knowledge as a path to truth.

passages.text: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
He who holds nothing dear and nothing hateful, he who has ceased former hatred and is not attached to new ones—he attains freedom.
Whenever there is a decline of righteousness and a rise of unrighteousness, O Arjuna, I manifest Myself.
passages.chapterTitle: Jnana Karma Sannyasa Yoga (The Yoga of Wisdom and Renunciation)

passages.text: Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

passages.text: There is no compulsion in religion. The right course has become distinct from the wrong.

scienceNotes: Psychological wisdom research (Baltes & Staudinger, 2000; Berlin Wisdom Paradigm) operationalizes wisdom as rich factual/procedural knowledge, lifespan contextualism, value relativism, and recognition of uncertainty. fMRI studies (Meeks & Jeste, 2009) associate wisdom with prefrontal cortex function, emotional regulation, and balanced dopamine/serotonin systems. Longitudinal data (Ardelt, 2004) finds wisdom predicts life satisfaction and psychological well-being in older adults independent of education and IQ. The integration of cognitive and affective processing — a core theme in wisdom traditions — is now a focus in affective neuroscience.

name: Justice & Righteousness
description: The principles of fairness, justice, and living righteously according to divine law.

passages.text: O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm for Allah, witnesses in justice, and do not let the hatred of a people cause you not to be just. Be just; that is nearer to righteousness. And fear Allah; indeed, Allah is Acquainted with what you do.

And now, Israel, what doth the LORD thy God require of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all his ways, and to love him, and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul,
Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.
Abandon all varieties of dharmas and surrender unto Me alone. I shall liberate you from all sins; do not grieve.
passages.chapterTitle: Moksha Sannyasa Yoga (The Yoga of Liberation and Renunciation)

passages.text: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who harbor such thoughts do not still their hatred.

scienceNotes: The neuroscience of fairness (Sanfey et al., 2003, Science) uses the Ultimatum Game to show that perceived unfairness activates the anterior insula (disgust response) and dorsolateral prefrontal cortex (cognitive control). Developmental psychology (Turiel, 2002) finds that justice reasoning emerges in children as young as 3-4 years, independent of religious instruction. Political philosophy's empirical turn (Haidt, 2012) identifies fairness/reciprocity as one of five innate moral foundations shared across cultures. Restorative justice programs (Latimer et al., 2005 meta-analysis) show higher offender accountability and victim satisfaction than punitive justice approaches.

name: Faith & Trust in God
description: The role of faith, trust, and belief in the divine as a foundation for spiritual life.

passages.text: To those who worship Me with devotion, meditating on Me, I give what they lack and preserve what they have.
passages.chapterTitle: Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (The Yoga of Sovereign Knowledge and Secret)

passages.text: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
Allah does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It will have [the consequence of] what [good] it has gained, and it will bear [the consequence of] what [evil] it has earned.
Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD.
Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

scienceNotes: The cognitive science of religion (Barrett, 2000; Boyer, 2001) proposes that religious belief arises from evolved cognitive mechanisms: agency detection, theory of mind, and teleological reasoning — all of which develop spontaneously in early childhood. fMRI studies of prayer and meditation (Schjoedt et al., 2009) show reduced frontal lobe activity during ritualized prayer consistent with automated cognitive processing. Psychologically, faith correlates with higher reported well-being (Koenig, 2012 meta-analysis of 850+ studies), though the causal direction is debated — social support and behavioral regulation are plausible mediators rather than belief per se.

name: Humility & Service
description: The virtue of humility, putting others before oneself, and serving others as a spiritual practice.

passages.text: He by whom the world is not troubled and who is not troubled by the world, who is free from joy and envy, fear and anxiety—he is dear to Me.
Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
Walk as if you are kissing the earth with your feet. Be steadfast, devoted, firm in virtue, gentle in spirit, humble.
Ik Onkar – There is One God. Truth is His Name. He is the Creator. He is without fear. He is without hatred. Timeless is His form. Beyond birth. Self-existent. By Guru's grace.
passages.chapterTitle: Japji Sahib — The Morning Prayer (Guru Nanak)

scienceNotes: The psychological science of intellectual humility (Leary et al., 2017; Porter & Schumann, 2018) shows it predicts receptivity to opposing views, willingness to revise beliefs, and reduced polarization. Neuroimaging (Zmigrod et al., 2019) associates dogmatism with reduced prefrontal flexibility. Leadership research (Collins, 2001, Good to Great) identified personal humility combined with professional will — 'Level 5 Leadership' — as a predictor of sustained organizational success. Social psychology finds that humble individuals are rated as more competent and likeable by peers, countering the assertiveness-default assumption.

name: Suffering & Endurance
description: Teachings on the nature of suffering, its causes, and how to endure and transcend it.

passages.text: Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with an impure mind a person speaks or acts, suffering follows him like the wheel that follows the foot of the ox.
And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away.
He whose mind is untroubled in the midst of sorrows and is free from eager desire amid pleasures, he from whom passion, fear, and rage have passed away—he is called a sage of steady wisdom.
For indeed, with hardship [will be] ease.
I call heaven and earth to record this day against you, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and cursing: therefore choose life, that both thou and thy seed may live.

scienceNotes: Pain neuroscience (Melzack, 1999; Tracey & Mantyh, 2007) distinguishes between nociception (sensory) and suffering (emotional/cognitive appraisal), mediated by the anterior cingulate cortex and insula. The neuromatrix theory of pain explains why meaning-making — including spiritual reframing of suffering — can modulate pain perception. Post-traumatic growth research (Tedeschi & Calhoun, 2004) finds 30-70% of trauma survivors report positive psychological changes, including deepened meaning, which aligns with scriptural narratives of redemptive suffering. Placebo-controlled studies show that mindfulness-based pain management reduces pain intensity by 22-33% (Zeidan et al., 2011).

name: Love & Devotion
description: The concept of divine love, loving God, and the importance of love in spiritual life.

passages.text: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
Or have they taken protectors [or allies] besides Him? For Allah — He is the Protector, and He gives life to the dead, and He is over all things competent.
Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give away, whatever austerities you perform—do that as an offering unto Me.
passages.chapterTitle: Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (The Yoga of Sovereign Knowledge and Secret)

passages.text: I am Thine, O Lord, I am Thine; all mine are Thine, O Great King. My body, my breath, my wealth and my soul are Thine, O Lord; everything is Thine.
passages.chapterTitle: Anand Sahib — The Song of Bliss (Guru Amar Das)

passages.text: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD.

scienceNotes: Attachment theory (Bowlby, 1969; Hazan & Shaver, 1987) provides an evolutionary framework: romantic love co-opts the mother-infant attachment system. fMRI studies (Bartels & Zeki, 2000; Fisher et al., 2005) show that romantic love activates dopamine-rich reward regions (caudate, ventral tegmental area) while deactivating critical social judgment areas (prefrontal cortex, amygdala). Long-term compassionate love — distinct from passionate love — correlates with activation in the ventral pallidum, associated with long-term pair bonding (Acevedo et al., 2012). The 'love hormone' oxytocin facilitates trust, empathy, and pair bonding across mammalian species.
